free_text: obtained by deuteron irradiation of Ta up to 50 MeV
High purity metalic natural Ta foils 8.63 um
The foils were arranged in a 20 times repeated sequence
Irradiation was done in a Faraday-cup like target
holder with 50 MeV, 100 nA deuteron beam for 60 min.
Four series of gamma measurements were performed with
cooling times of 5-6 h, 24-48 h, 4-7 d and 60-80 d.
Additional measurements with extended range Ge-detector
for X-rays were taken after 90-199 d cooling time.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2 and Lund/LBNL online
The 48.72 MeV initial beam energy and 100 nA beam
intensity was adopted in the data analysis as a good
agreement was found between the re-measured excitation
function and the recommended monitor reactions.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of energy loss of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam
energy was +- 0.2 MeV which increased up to +- 2.2 MeV
in the last foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
ts. Zero ERR-T values in subentries 002 and
ts. Zero ERR-T values at 39.1 MeV were
